questions.question: What is the purpose of the USB HID description in Linux?
questions.justification: The synthesis text states that the USB HID description is a mechanism to describe the human interface characteristics of USB devices, providing a standardized way to define how USB devices interact with the operating system and applications.

questions.question: How can Linux systems access and parse the USB HID description?
questions.justification: The synthesis text explains that Linux systems can access and parse the USB HID description through the `hidraw` kernel subsystem, and provides an example code snippet demonstrating the use of the `ioctl()` system call to read the HID report descriptor.

questions.question: What are the common HID items defined in the HID report descriptor?
questions.justification: The synthesis text mentions some common HID items like `Collection` and `Report`, but does not provide a comprehensive list of all the common HID items.
